response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software engineer with strong web/full-stack and some systems-level background, but their professional C++ exposure is minimal and dated (a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a university thesis). The role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise, industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443, SDL), and MQTT/IoT protocol experience, none of which appear on the resume. Their low-latency DLang work shows aptitude for concurrent, high-performance systems, but the core skill match is too weak for a senior C++ role in industrial automation.
strengths: High-performance, low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic, albeit in DLang
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing culture, mentoring and workshop/teaching experience
Broad multi-domain background (web, mobile, embedded, GIS) with event-driven platform design
concerns: Very limited and outdated professional C++ experience; recent decade is mostly TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity, SDL, IEC 62443, or protocols like MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA
No demonstrated experience with message brokers at scale in the industrial/IoT context the role targets
